pattern: Each star cancels the most recently surviving character, which is exactly the behavior of a stack: push letters and pop on a star.
hints: A star always deletes the nearest letter still standing to its left, so think about what 'nearest surviving' means.
Process characters left to right, keeping the survivors in a structure where the most recent one is easiest to remove.
Use a stack: push every letter, and on a star pop the top; the remaining stack, read bottom to top, is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: letter then star then more letters

stress.description: random valid star string where stars never exceed prior letters
stress.note: construction guarantees every star has a letter to remove
